maxContractTerm.value: 5 years (hard statutory cap). SDCL 5-18C-1: 'No contract for the transportation of students may exceed five years. Specific provisions of the contract may be renegotiated during the term of the contract if guidelines for making changes are in the contract. Any change made during the term of the contract shall be reported in the school board minutes.' No statutory authorization to extend beyond five years via renewal options; only mid-term renegotiation of specific provisions is allowed (and only where guidelines for changes are in the contract, with any change recorded in school-board minutes).
maxContractTerm.citation: SDCL 5-18C-1 (Contract for transportation of students), Source: SL 2010, ch 31, § 85. Official URL: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18C-1 (API: https://sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/5-18C-1.html).

rebidRequired.label: Conditional
rebidRequired.rule: South Dakota does NOT statutorily mandate competitive (sealed-bid / RFP) rebidding of student-transportation contracts at term-end. SDCL 5-18A-22(12) (renumbered as (13) effective July 1, 2026) expressly exempts 'Any purchase by a school district of perishable food, raw materials used in construction or manufacture of products for resale, or for transportation of students' from the public-agency competitive-bidding requirements of chapters 5-18A, 5-18B, 5-18C, and 5-18D. Because SDCL 5-18C-1 caps any single transportation contract at five years, the district must enter into a new contract at the end of the term (the existing contract cannot run longer than five years), but state law does not require that re-award to be made through a competitive process. If a district nonetheless elects to use public bidding for transportation services, SDCL 13-29-20 separately prohibits the school district itself from participating as a bidder in that public-bidding process. The official SD Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet (July 1, 2024, p. 6, item 'g') confirms both elements: the SDCL 5-18A-22(12) exemption and the SDCL 5-18C-1 five-year cap, quoted side-by-side.
rebidRequired.citation: SDCL 5-18A-22(12) [renumbered (13) eff. July 1, 2026] (Procurements exempt from chapters 5-18A through 5-18D); SDCL 5-18C-1 (five-year cap); SDCL 13-29-20 (district may not bid as a contractor); SD Department of Legislative Audit, 'South Dakota Local Government Guide for Acquisitions, Disposals and Exchanges' (Bid Booklet), July 1, 2024, p. 6 (Exemptions from Bidding, item g). Official URLs: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18A-22 ; https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18C-1 ; https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/13-29-20 ; https://legislativeaudit.sd.gov/resources/Bid%20Booklet%202024.pdf.

renewalMechanics.value: Renewal beyond the five-year cap is not statutorily authorized. SDCL 5-18C-1 contains no analogue to SDCL 5-18C-2 (food service), which expressly permits a one-year initial term plus up to four consecutive annual renewals; the absence of an analogous express renewal mechanic in 5-18C-1 is the negative-implication confirmation that no statutory renewal mechanic exists for student-transportation contracts. Within the five-year term, SDCL 5-18C-1 permits the parties to renegotiate 'specific provisions of the contract ... if guidelines for making changes are in the contract,' and 'Any change made during the term of the contract shall be reported in the school board minutes.' That is the only statutorily described mid-term change mechanism. The statute does not provide for CPI / fuel-escalator clauses, evergreen / automatic rollover, or state-agency approval of renewals; any such mechanic would have to be drafted into the contract itself within the five-year ceiling. At term end, a new contract must be entered (the existing contract cannot exceed five years); because SDCL 5-18A-22(12) exempts transportation from competitive bidding, the new contract may, but need not, be competitively procured.
renewalMechanics.citation: SDCL 5-18C-1 (renegotiation of specific provisions during the term per contract guidelines; changes reported in school-board minutes); contrast SDCL 5-18C-2 (food service one-year term plus four annual renewals); SDCL 5-18A-22(12) [renumbered (13) eff. July 1, 2026] (transportation of students exempt from competitive bidding). Official URLs: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18C-1 ; https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18C-2 ; https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18A-22.

finalFields.statutoryCitation: SDCL 5-18C-1 (Contract for transportation of students; five-year cap and in-term renegotiation mechanic) - primary, transportation-specific. Source: SL 2010, ch 31, § 85. Official URL: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18C-1. Supplemented by: SDCL 5-18A-22(12) [renumbered (13) eff. July 1, 2026] (school-district transportation exempt from competitive-bidding chapters 5-18A through 5-18D; current source: SL 2010, ch 31, § 23 with amendments through SL 2024, ch 1 (Ex. Ord. 24-1), § 34, eff. Apr. 8, 2024; renumbering: SL 2026, ch 29, § 1, eff. July 1, 2026) - Official URL: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/5-18A-22; SDCL 13-29-20 (school district prohibited from being a bidder where public bidding is used; Source: SL 1994, ch 123; SL 1996, ch 110, § 19) - Official URL: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/13-29-20; SDCL 13-20-3 (channels school-district construction / supplies / equipment procurement through 5-18A and 5-18B) - Official URL: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/13-20-3; SDCL 13-29-1 (school board may acquire, own, operate, or hire buses; charter bus contracts) - Official URL: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/13-29-1; SDCL 13-37-8.9 (special-education transportation: parent mileage; or district may pay actual cost of common-carrier transportation or of bus service provided by contract with the facility in which the child is enrolled) - Official URL: https://sdlegislature.gov/Statutes/13-37-8.9. Confirmed by official SD Department of Legislative Audit, 'South Dakota Local Government Guide for Acquisitions, Disposals and Exchanges' (Bid Booklet), July 1, 2024, p. 6 - Official URL: https://legislativeaudit.sd.gov/resources/Bid%20Booklet%202024.pdf.

districtVariance.label: Unknown
districtVariance.explanation: Official statewide guidance (SD Department of Legislative Audit, Bid Booklet, July 1, 2024) treats SDCL 5-18C-1's five-year cap and the SDCL 5-18A-22(12) bidding exemption for 'transportation of students' as uniform rules applicable to all school districts, with no district-by-district variation in the rule itself. ASBSD (the school boards association) maintains sample policies DJ (Purchasing Procedures) and DJC (Bidding Requirements) consistent with the same statutory framework. No SD Attorney General opinion, state-auditor finding, or DOE bulletin was located indicating that districts interpret the five-year cap or the bidding exemption differently. However, no comprehensive cross-district survey of pupil-transportation contracting practice was located either, so the answer remains 'Unknown' rather than 'No' under the standard that 'No' requires affirmative statewide-uniformity evidence. The statute's plain text (a five-year hard ceiling and a clean exemption from bidding) leaves limited room for interpretive variance on the load-bearing legal points, but district practice on whether to rebid voluntarily, use cooperative purchasing, or roll over within the five-year cap was not surveyed.

specialEducation.detail: South Dakota law does NOT set different contract-term limits, rebid requirements, procurement rules, or renewal mechanics for special-education transportation, IEP-mandated transportation, McKinney-Vento transportation, foster-care transportation, or alternative student transportation. SDCL 5-18C-1 (the five-year cap) speaks to 'transportation of students' without distinguishing special education. SDCL 5-18A-22(12) likewise exempts a school district's purchase 'for transportation of students' without distinguishing special education. SDCL 13-37-8.9 (within the special-education chapter, Chapter 13-37) addresses how special-education transportation may be paid - including, in lieu of parent mileage, 'the actual cost of transportation by common carrier or of bus service provided by contract with the facility in which the child is enrolled' - and provides a parent mileage allowance (no less than the state mileage rate under SDCL 3-9-1) when district bus service is not available, but it does not impose a different term limit or procurement regime on the contract itself. Federal IDEA and McKinney-Vento law impose duties to provide transportation but do not, by themselves, alter SD's state-law contract-term or competitive-bidding regime; that is a federal-duty-to-transport overlay, not a SD contract or procurement rule. No SD ARSD provision (including the 24:05 special-education rules) was located setting a special-education-specific contract-term or competitive-bidding rule for transportation contracts.

perFieldVerdicts.note: SDCL 5-18C-1 re-opened on the official SD Legislature API (https://sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/5-18C-1.html). Full verbatim text - including the five-year cap, the mid-term renegotiation sentence, and the school-board-minutes sentence - matches Stage 1's quotation exactly. Source: SL 2010, ch 31, § 85; no subsequent amendments. No statutory extension/renewal-option authority or grandfathering provision was located in challenge searches.

SDCL 5-18A-22 re-opened on the official SD Legislature API; subdivision (12) verbatim matches Stage 1 (renumbered to (13) effective July 1, 2026 per SL 2026, ch 29, § 1 with no substantive change). SDCL 13-29-20 re-opened verbatim. The Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet (July 1, 2024) was re-downloaded and parsed: page 6 item (g) reads exactly 'Any purchase by a school district of perishable food, raw materials used in construction or manufacture of products for resale, or for transportation of students. (SDCL 5-18A-22 (12)) / No contract for the transportation of students may exceed five years. (SDCL 5-18C-1)' - verbatim confirmation of the Conditional label. Disconfirming challenges (searching for a mandatory competitive-bidding requirement for student transportation in any school, procurement, or local-government chapter) returned no statute imposing one.

5-18C-1 contains only the in-term renegotiation mechanic and the school-board-minutes reporting requirement; no extension, evergreen, CPI, or fuel-escalator authority is present in the statute. SDCL 5-18C-2 (food service) re-opened verbatim - its express one-year-plus-four-annual-renewals mechanic stands in conspicuous contrast to 5-18C-1 and reinforces the negative inference. Disconfirming searches for renewal-option, extension, multi-year, successive, or notwithstanding authority for student-transportation contracts returned no statutory authority.

All primary citations (5-18C-1, 5-18A-22, 13-29-20) and all supporting citations (5-18C-2, 13-20-3, 13-29-1, 13-37-8.9) re-opened verbatim on the official SD Legislature API. The Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet (July 1, 2024) was independently re-fetched and parsed. No citation in the Stage 1 pack failed re-verification.

Stage 1's 'Unknown' label survives challenge. No comprehensive cross-district survey of pupil-transportation contracting practice was located, and no SD AG opinion, state-auditor finding, or DOE bulletin indicating non-uniform interpretation was located. ASBSD Purchasing/Bidding/Bonds page confirmed: it discusses sample policies DJ and DJC consistent with the statewide framework (and references a SD Supreme Court case, Wessington Springs Ed. Assoc. v. Wessington Springs Sch. Dist., 467 N.W.2d 101 (S.D. 1991)), but is not itself a survey of district variance. Statewide guidance treats the rule uniformly; threshold for 'No' (affirmative evidence of statewide uniform district interpretation) not met; 'Unknown' is correct.

SDCL 13-37-8.9 re-opened verbatim: confirms that special-education transportation payment options (parent mileage allowance / common carrier / bus service by contract with the receiving facility) are addressed, but no different contract-term or procurement rule is set. SDCL 5-18C-1 and SDCL 5-18A-22(12) both speak to 'transportation of students' without distinguishing special education. No ARSD provision (including 24:05 special-education rules) imposing a special-education-specific contract-term or competitive-bidding rule was located.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: 5-year hard statutory cap under SDCL 5-18C-1.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: There is a 'notwithstanding', 'may renew', 'extension', 'option', 'successive', or 'multi-year' authority somewhere in SD law that lets a school district contractually agree to a transportation-services contract longer than five years (e.g., a 5+5 renewal-option structure, or an automatic-rollover/evergreen provision).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (a) Re-fetched SDCL 5-18C-1 verbatim on the official SD Legislature API; full text limits any single contract to five years and contains no renewal-option, extension, multi-year, or notwithstanding language. (b) WebSearch for 'South Dakota "transportation of students" contract "renewal option" OR "extension" OR "successive" SDCL'. (c) Reviewed adjacent SDCL 5-18C sections (5-18C-2 through 5-18C-8): none authorize an extension to the student-transportation cap; 5-18C-2 (food service) is the only section in Chapter 5-18C with an express renewal mechanic and it applies to a different subject. (d) Reviewed SDCL Chapter 13-29 (School Buses and Transportation of Students) section-by-section: no extension/renewal-option authority for contracts subject to the 5-18C-1 cap. (e) Confirmed Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet (July 1, 2024) restates the same cap without any extension authority.
challengeMatrix.result: No counter-authority located. Five-year hard cap stands as Stage 1 stated.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid at Term-End: Conditional - no statutory rebidding mandate; SDCL 5-18A-22(12) exempts transportation from competitive bidding; but contract must be re-let because of the 5-year cap.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A different SDCL chapter (or an administrative rule) imposes mandatory competitive bidding on school-district transportation contracts, overriding the 5-18A-22(12) exemption (e.g., a school-specific bidding mandate in Chapter 13-20 or a procurement-manual rule).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (a) Re-fetched SDCL 5-18A-22 (full text) on the official API; subdivision (12) verbatim exempts the school-district purchase 'for transportation of students' from chapters 5-18A through 5-18D. (b) Re-fetched SDCL 13-20-3 verbatim; it channels school-district construction/supplies/equipment procurement through 5-18A and 5-18B but does not override the 5-18A-22(12) exemption for transportation services. (c) WebSearch for 'SDCL "school district" "transportation services" "competitive bidding" required OR mandate' - returned no override authority. (d) Reviewed Bid Booklet (Department of Legislative Audit, July 1, 2024) page 6 item (g) which restates the exemption verbatim and ties it directly to the 5-year cap, confirming this is the operative reading. (e) Re-verified SDCL 13-29-20 verbatim: bars the district from being a bidder if public bidding is used, but does not require public bidding.
challengeMatrix.result: No mandatory-bidding override located. 'Conditional' label stands.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: Only mid-term renegotiation under 5-18C-1; no statutory renewal-option, CPI, fuel-escalator, evergreen, or state-agency-approval mechanic.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Either (i) SDCL 5-18C-1 contains, or some other statute supplies, an express renewal-option, CPI/fuel-escalator, or automatic-rollover mechanic; OR (ii) an ARSD rule supplies such mechanic.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (a) Re-fetched full text of SDCL 5-18C-1: contains only the in-term renegotiation sentence and the school-board-minutes reporting sentence; no CPI, fuel, escalator, evergreen, extension, or successive-term language. (b) Side-by-side compared SDCL 5-18C-2 (food service): expressly authorizes 'one year' plus 'no more than four [annual] renewals' - the SD Legislature plainly knows how to write a renewal mechanic and conspicuously omitted one from 5-18C-1. (c) WebSearch for '"5-18C-1" "renegotiated" "school board minutes" "guidelines for making changes"' - returned no contrary authority. (d) Reviewed Bid Booklet section 'Written Contracts' and 'Additional Requirements of School Districts'; no transportation-specific renewal mechanic supplied. (e) Spot-checked SDCL Chapter 13-29 for any renewal-mechanic statute applicable to transportation contracts; none located.
challengeMatrix.result: No additional statutory renewal mechanic located. Renewal-mechanics finding stands.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-Education Differentiation: No - same statutory regime applies to special-education transportation contracts as to regular-route transportation.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A separate statute or ARSD provision sets a different contract-term, procurement rule, or renewal mechanic for special-education, IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, homeless-student, or disability transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (a) Re-fetched full text of SDCL 13-37-8.9 (within the special-education chapter): addresses parent mileage allowance, common-carrier reimbursement, and 'bus service provided by contract with the facility in which the child is enrolled' as payment options, but sets no different contract-term or procurement regime. (b) WebSearch for 'South Dakota special education transportation contract procurement ARSD "24:43" OR "24:05" IEP' - returned ARSD 24:05 (special-education rules) which addresses related services including transportation but does not impose a different contract-term or competitive-bidding rule for the contract itself. (c) Reviewed SDCL Chapter 13-37 generally for any procurement override applicable to special-education transportation contracts; none located. (d) Searched for McKinney-Vento and foster-care transportation contracting overlays in SDCL; none located.
challengeMatrix.result: No special-education-specific contract or procurement rule located. Stage 1's 'No' (same regime applies) stands.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: District Variance: Unknown - statewide guidance treats the rules as uniform, but no comprehensive cross-district survey was located.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Either (i) authoritative evidence of uniform district interpretation exists (which would justify 'No'); OR (ii) authoritative evidence of inconsistent district interpretation exists (which would justify 'Yes').
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (a) Re-fetched ASBSD Purchasing/Bidding/Bonds page (https://asbsd.org/pa-purchasing-bidding-bonds/); confirms sample policies DJ and DJC consistent with the statewide framework but is not a district-by-district survey. (b) Reviewed Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet for any commentary on inter-district variance; the Booklet treats the rules as uniform but does not survey practice. (c) WebSearch for '"Sioux Falls" OR "Rapid City" school district transportation contract RFP "five years" bid term' - returned bid-portal links but no published district-by-district comparative study or auditor finding describing inconsistent interpretation. (d) Searched for SD AG opinions and state-auditor reports addressing district variance in transportation contracting; none located.
challengeMatrix.result: No authoritative cross-district survey located; statewide guidance is uniform but the threshold for 'No' is not met. 'Unknown' stands.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Confirmed; Unknown is the correct neutral-recorder label.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness: Stage 1 reads the law as current through Apr. 8, 2024 (5-18A-22) and asserts the July 1, 2026 amendment is purely renumbering.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 2025 or 2026 session-law amendment changed the substance of the 5-year cap (5-18C-1), the transportation exemption (5-18A-22), the bidder bar (13-29-20), or repealed any of the cited sections.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (a) Re-fetched SDCL 5-18C-1, 5-18A-22, and 13-29-20 on the official SD Legislature API and read the Source/session-law lines at the bottom. 5-18C-1 source line: 'SL 2010, ch 31, § 85' - no subsequent amendment. 5-18A-22 source line: extends through 'SL 2024, ch 1 (Ex. Ord. 24-1), § 34, eff. Apr. 8, 2024' and 'SL 2026, ch 29, § 1' (the renumbering amendment, eff. July 1, 2026, moves 'transportation of students' from subdivision (12) to (13) - substance unchanged). 13-29-20 source line: 'SL 1994, ch 123; SL 1996, ch 110, § 19' - no subsequent amendment. (b) WebSearch 'South Dakota 2025 OR 2026 legislative session bill "transportation of students" OR "pupil transportation" amendment' - returned no bill repealing or substantively amending the cited sections.
challengeMatrix.result: No substantive amendment after the recorded code-currency date. Currentness confirmed.

SDCL 13-29-20 (School districts prohibited from bidding to provide student transportation services) - verbatim text confirmed via https://sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/13-29-20.html; Source: SL 1994, ch 123; SL 1996, ch 110, § 19.

SDCL 5-18C-2 (Contract for services of school food service management company) - verbatim text confirmed via https://sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/5-18C.html?all=true; Source: SL 2010, ch 31, § 86 (cited for negative-implication contrast only).
SDCL 13-20-3 (Contracts let in accordance with public agency procurement law) - verbatim text confirmed via https://sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/13-20-3.html.
SDCL 13-29-1 (District operation of buses permitted - Purposes for which used) - verbatim text confirmed via https://sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/13-29-1.html.
SDCL 13-37-8.9 (Mileage allowance in lieu of transportation for special education) - verbatim text confirmed via https://sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/13-37-8.9.html; Source: SL 2007, ch 105, § 1.

SD Department of Legislative Audit, 'South Dakota Local Government Guide for Acquisitions, Disposals and Exchanges' (Bid Booklet), July 1, 2024 - downloaded and parsed page 6 item (g) verbatim: 'Any purchase by a school district of perishable food, raw materials used in construction or manufacture of products for resale, or for transportation of students. (SDCL 5-18A-22 (12)) / No contract for the transportation of students may exceed five years. (SDCL 5-18C-1)' - https://legislativeaudit.sd.gov/resources/Bid%20Booklet%202024.pdf.

sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Codified Laws current; Source: SL 2010, ch 31, § 85 (no subsequent amendment).
sourceLedger.relevantText: Verbatim: 'No contract for the transportation of students may exceed five years. Specific provisions of the contract may be renegotiated during the term of the contract if guidelines for making changes are in the contract. Any change made during the term of the contract shall be reported in the school board minutes.'

sourceLedger.citation: SDCL 5-18A-22 (Procurements exempt from chapters 5-18A through 5-18D), subdivision (12) [current] / (13) [eff. July 1, 2026]
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current text through SL 2024, ch 1 (Ex. Ord. 24-1), § 34, eff. Apr. 8, 2024; future version effective July 1, 2026 per SL 2026, ch 29, § 1 - renumbering only, substance unchanged.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 2 (Rebid Required) - statutory exemption from competitive bidding.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Verbatim (subdivision (12) [current] / (13) [eff. July 1, 2026]): 'Any purchase by a school district of perishable food, raw materials used in construction or manufacture of products for resale, or for transportation of students.'

sourceLedger.citation: SDCL 13-29-20 (School districts prohibited from bidding to provide student transportation services)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Codified Laws current; Source: SL 1994, ch 123; SL 1996, ch 110, § 19.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 2 (Rebid Required) - bar on the school district acting as a bidder when public bidding is used.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Verbatim: 'No school district may participate as a bidder in the public bidding conducted for the purpose of providing transportation services for students to attend school in the district.'

sourceLedger.citation: SDCL 5-18C-2 (Contract for services of school food service management company) - cited for negative-implication contrast
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Codified Laws current; Source: SL 2010, ch 31, § 86.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Verbatim (excerpt): 'No contract for the services of a local school food service management company may exceed one year. An original contract for the services of a school food service management company may be renewed annually no more than four times consecutively following the original contract. Both bid and contract shall specify that the contract may be renewed, but the local school is not required to renew the contract.'

sourceLedger.citation: SDCL 13-20-3 (Contracts let in accordance with public agency procurement law)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Codified Laws current; Source: through SL 2011, ch 2, § 124.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 4 (Statutory Citation) - school-board contracting framework reference.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Verbatim (excerpt): 'if any school facilities are to be built or remodeled, or improvements are to be made to school sites, or if supplies or equipment are to be purchased, the school board shall let contracts in accordance with chapters 5-18A and 5-18B...' Services such as transportation are subject to 5-18A and the 5-18C-1 specific rule, subject to the 5-18A-22(12) exemption.

sourceLedger.citation: SDCL 13-29-1 (District operation of buses permitted - Purposes for which used)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Codified Laws current; latest amendment SL 2000, ch 85, § 1 and ch 86, § 1.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 4 (Statutory Citation) - general authority of school board to acquire, own, operate, or hire buses, including via contract with federally regulated charter bus operations.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Verbatim (excerpt): 'The school board of any school district may acquire, own, operate, or hire buses for the transportation of students to and from its schools... The school district may contract with a federally regulated charter bus operation to provide charter bus service...'

sourceLedger.citation: SDCL 13-37-8.9 (Mileage allowance in lieu of transportation for special education)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Codified Laws current; Source: SL 2007, ch 105, § 1.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education differentiation - confirms no different procurement regime; addresses payment options only.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Verbatim (excerpt): 'If appropriate bus service is not provided by a school district, the parents or guardian of any child in need of special education or special education and related services, when legally assigned, are eligible for necessary transportation compensation for their child as determined by the individualized education program team... In lieu of compensation for mileage, a district may pay the actual cost of transportation by common carrier or of bus service provided by contract with the facility in which the child is enrolled.'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 4 - Official state-agency / procurement-agency guidance citing binding statutes
sourceLedger.citation: South Dakota Department of Legislative Audit, 'South Dakota Local Government Guide for Acquisitions, Disposals and Exchanges' (Bid Booklet), July 1, 2024
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Prepared July 1, 2024 by SD Department of Legislative Audit.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 1, 2, 3, 4 - confirms statutory reading on the five-year cap and the bidding exemption; Field 5 - statewide guidance treats rules uniformly but is not a district-by-district survey.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Page 6 item (g) under 'Exemptions from Bidding' (verbatim): 'Any purchase by a school district of perishable food, raw materials used in construction or manufacture of products for resale, or for transportation of students. (SDCL 5-18A-22 (12))' Followed immediately by: 'No contract for the transportation of students may exceed five years. (SDCL 5-18C-1)'.

sourceLedger.citation: Associated School Boards of South Dakota (ASBSD), 'Policy Alert: Purchasing, Bidding and Bonds' (sample policies DJ, DJC, FEFB framework)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Sample-policy framework published by ASBSD; consistent with current SDCL 5-18A / 5-18B / 5-18C framework. References Wessington Springs Ed. Assoc. v. Wessington Springs Sch. Dist., 467 N.W.2d 101 (S.D. 1991).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 5 (district variance) - centralized sample-policy framework suggests centralized practice; does not itself document inter-district practice on transportation contract term or rebidding.
sourceLedger.relevantText: ASBSD provides sample policies DJ (Purchasing Procedures) and DJC (Bidding Requirements) - covering Definitions, Competitive Sealed Bids, Competitive Sealed Proposals, Advertising for Bids and Proposals, Awarding Contracts, and Contracts Exempt from Bidding - to member school districts.

sourceLedger.citation: South Dakota Department of Education, Office of Accreditation, Technology and Quality - School Transportation page
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education / general transportation - confirms DOE guidance addresses driver training, bus inspections, and licensing (citing SDCL 13-29-21); no DOE rule sets a different contract-term or procurement regime for transportation contracts.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Page addresses cooperative administration with Highway Patrol and Driver Licensing Office; construction standards set by Board of Education Standards; annual reporting of school bus drivers; safety inspections and licensing/testing; mandatory driver training every five years per SDCL 13-29-21. No contract-term or procurement-process rule for student-transportation contracts is set by DOE guidance.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Adversarial-validation pass for South Dakota only. Started by re-opening every primary citation in Stage 1's pack on the official SD Legislature API (sdlegislature.gov/api/Statutes/{section}.html) - the official site's /Statutes/{section} HTML pages return a browser-check page to WebFetch, but the /api/Statutes/{section}.html endpoints return the verbatim statute text and source/session-law lines reliably. Re-fetched 5-18C-1, 5-18A-22, 13-29-20, 5-18C-2, 13-20-3, 13-29-1, and 13-37-8.9 - all matched Stage 1's quotations exactly. Independently re-downloaded the Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet (July 1, 2024) PDF and parsed it with pdftotext; page 6 item (g) verbatim matches Stage 1's quotation. ASBSD Policy Alert page and the SD DOE school-transportation page were re-fetched; both confirm the statewide framework Stage 1 described. Disconfirming searches used the following queries (and produced no counter-authority): 'South Dakota "transportation of students" contract "renewal option" OR "extension" OR "successive" SDCL'; 'SDCL "school district" "transportation services" "competitive bidding" required OR mandate'; 'South Dakota special education transportation contract procurement ARSD "24:43" OR "24:05" IEP'; '"South Dakota" attorney general opinion school transportation contract bidding 5-18C'; 'South Dakota 2025 OR 2026 legislative session bill "transportation of students" OR "pupil transportation" amendment'; '"Sioux Falls" OR "Rapid City" school district transportation contract RFP "five years" bid term'.
verificationNotes.currentLawCheck: SDCL 5-18C-1 source line is 'SL 2010, ch 31, § 85' with no subsequent amendment - section unchanged since enactment. SDCL 5-18A-22 source line extends through 'SL 2024, ch 1 (Ex. Ord. 24-1), § 34, eff. Apr. 8, 2024' (current) and 'SL 2026, ch 29, § 1' (renumbering only, effective July 1, 2026). The renumbering moves the 'transportation of students' exemption from subdivision (12) to subdivision (13) - substance unchanged. SDCL 13-29-20 source line is 'SL 1994, ch 123; SL 1996, ch 110, § 19' - no subsequent amendment. Administrative-code check: ARSD 24:05 (special-education rules) addresses transportation as a related service but does not set a contract-term or competitive-bidding rule for transportation contracts; no other ARSD provision was located setting a transportation-contracting rule. SD DOE School Transportation guidance is limited to driver training, safety inspections, and licensing. 2025 and 2026 session-law searches surfaced no bill substantively altering SDCL 5-18C-1, the 5-18A-22 transportation exemption, or 13-29-20.
verificationNotes.unresolvedIssues: District-level interpretation variance (Field 5): no comprehensive cross-district survey of South Dakota school-district pupil-transportation contracting practice was located despite searches for SD AG opinions, state-auditor reports, ASBSD policy materials, and large-district RFPs (Sioux Falls, Rapid City). Statewide official guidance (Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet, ASBSD sample policies) treats the rules uniformly, but the threshold for 'No' (affirmative evidence of statewide uniform district interpretation across districts as a matter of practice) was not met, so 'Unknown' is the correct neutral-recorder label rather than 'No'.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No authoritative conflict was located. The transportation-specific rule (SDCL 5-18C-1) and the general procurement-exemption rule (SDCL 5-18A-22(12)) read together harmoniously - they are not in conflict; the Bid Booklet (July 1, 2024) confirms the joint reading verbatim. No SDCL chapter imposing a mandatory competitive-bidding requirement on student-transportation contracts was located; SDCL 13-29-20 only bars the district itself from being a bidder where public bidding is used, and does not require public bidding. No special-education-specific procurement override was located. No SD AG opinion or state-auditor report contradicting the Stage 1 reading was located.

notesSpecialProvisions: South Dakota's student-transportation contracting regime is set by SDCL 5-18C-1 (a flat five-year cap on any single contract for transportation of students) operating in tandem with SDCL 5-18A-22(12) (renumbered to (13) effective July 1, 2026), which expressly exempts a school district's purchase 'for transportation of students' from the competitive-bidding requirements of chapters 5-18A, 5-18B, 5-18C, and 5-18D. The practical effect is that the district must enter into a new contract at the end of the five-year term (because no contract may exceed five years), but state law does not require the re-award to be made through a competitive sealed bid or RFP. If a district nonetheless chooses to use public bidding, SDCL 13-29-20 separately bars the school district itself from participating as a bidder. Within the five-year term, SDCL 5-18C-1 allows the parties to renegotiate 'specific provisions' if the contract contains guidelines for making such changes, and any change must be reported in the school-board minutes; the statute does not authorize CPI/fuel-escalator clauses, evergreen rollover, or state-agency approval mechanisms, so any such mechanic would have to be drafted into the contract itself within the five-year ceiling. The Department of Legislative Audit's 'Bid Booklet' (July 1, 2024) authoritatively states this joint reading on page 6, item (g). Other generally applicable procurement carve-outs (sole-source procurement under SDCL 5-18A-32, emergency procurement under SDCL 5-18A-32 and related provisions, cooperative/piggyback purchasing from another government's competitively awarded contract under SDCL 5-18A-22(3), state-contract-list purchases under SDCL 5-18C-8) are available to school districts as procurement tools, but the SDCL 5-18A-22(12) exemption already removes student-transportation contracts from the bidding regime, so those carve-outs are typically not needed for the procurement itself. Special-education transportation is contracted under the same regime: SDCL 5-18C-1 and SDCL 5-18A-22(12) both speak to 'transportation of students' without distinguishing special education; SDCL 13-37-8.9 addresses how special-education transportation may be paid (parent mileage allowance no less than the state mileage rate per SDCL 3-9-1; in lieu of mileage, common-carrier cost or 'bus service provided by contract with the facility in which the child is enrolled'), but imposes no different contract-term or competitive-bidding rule on the contract itself. No state-agency approval is required for the contract under SDCL 5-18C-1. ARSD 24:05 (special-education rules) addresses transportation as a related service but does not impose a separate contract-term or procurement rule on transportation contracts. The SD DOE School Transportation page (doe.sd.gov/oatq/transportation.aspx) confirms DOE's role is limited to driver training, safety inspections, and licensing (citing SDCL 13-29-21 on driver training every five years), not contract term or procurement. Charter-school procurement is not separately differentiated by SD statute for student-transportation contracts. ASBSD maintains sample policies DJ (Purchasing Procedures) and DJC (Bidding Requirements) consistent with the same statutory framework.
exceptionsUnresolved: Four of the five fields resolved with High confidence: Max Contract Term (five-year hard cap under SDCL 5-18C-1); Rebid Required at Term-End (Conditional - no statutory mandate because of the SDCL 5-18A-22(12) exemption, but a new contract must be entered at term-end because the existing contract cannot exceed five years; SDCL 13-29-20 bars the district from bidding if public bidding is used); Renewal Mechanics (no statutory renewal-option / CPI / fuel-escalator / evergreen / state-approval authority; only mid-term renegotiation per SDCL 5-18C-1's express terms); and Statutory Citation (SDCL 5-18C-1 plus 5-18A-22(12) plus 13-29-20, supplemented by 13-20-3, 13-29-1, and 13-37-8.9, all re-verified verbatim on the official SD Legislature API). The fifth field - District-Level Interpretation Variance - remains 'Unknown' (not 'No' and not 'Yes'). The statewide framework is uniform on its face: the Department of Legislative Audit Bid Booklet (July 1, 2024) restates the SDCL 5-18C-1 cap and the SDCL 5-18A-22(12) exemption as the operative rule for all school districts, and ASBSD maintains sample policies (DJ, DJC) consistent with that framework, but no comprehensive cross-district survey of pupil-transportation contracting practice was located. Disconfirming searches looked for SD AG opinions, state-auditor findings, and DOE bulletins describing inconsistent district interpretation, and for affirmative statewide-uniformity evidence at the practice level - neither side surfaced. Under the standard that 'No' requires affirmative evidence of statewide uniform district interpretation and 'Yes' requires credible evidence of inconsistent interpretation, 'Unknown' is the correct neutral-recorder label.
